pages.raw_text:
The members of the Washington del
egation in congress were not highly
successful during the session which has
lately closed in securing legislation
for the people of the state.


The five members of the Washington
delegation introduced, all told, lOS bills
in one house or the other during the
late session. At least four or five Of
these measures were of joint author
ship. OF the 1903 measures Is were
passed by both houses and became
laws.


Senator Ankeny introduced seven
bills, two of which became laws: Per-
uniting the erection of smelter on the
Colville Indian reservation, and to sell
Fort Walla Walla hay reservation.


Senator Foster introduced 20 bills,
the following five becoming laws:
Authorizing the sale of Puyallup lands,
granting part of public reservation for
city park at Port Angeles, allowing
vessels of less than 80 tons to engage
in British Columbia trade, appropria-
ting money for keepers dwelling at the
New Dungeness light house, validating
Northern Pacific land sales in right of
way.


Representative Cushman introduced
